name: GH_Path
summary: Describes the path in structure space of a data item or a list of items. A path consists of a series of integers, each one of which represents an index in a branch structure.

constructors.name: GH_Path()
constructors.summary: Default constructor, creates a path with zero elements.

constructors.name: GH_Path(Int32)
constructors.summary: Creates a new path with a single element.

params.name: index
params.doc: The index of the first element.

constructors.name: GH_Path(Int32[])
constructors.summary: Creates a new path from a series of elements.

params.name: args
params.doc: The index values of this path, if null, an empty path is created.

constructors.name: GH_Path(GH_Path)
constructors.summary: Creates an exact copy of another path.

params.name: Other
params.doc: Path to copy, cannot be null.

throws.when: Thrown if Other is a null reference

methods.name: AppendElement
methods.summary: Create a new path by appending a new index value to this path.

params.name: index
params.doc: Index value to append.

methods.name: Compare
methods.signature: public int Compare(GH_Path x, GH_Path y)
methods.summary: Compare two paths. This function determines the Sorting behaviour of paths.

methods.name: CompareTo
methods.summary: Compare this path to another path. This function determines the Sorting behaviour of paths.

methods.name: CullElement
methods.summary: Removes the last index value from the path. If the path is already empty, nothing will happen.

methods.name: CullFirstElement
methods.summary: Removes the first index value from the path. If the path is already empty, nothing will happen.

methods.name: Format
methods.signature: public string Format(string format_provider, string separator)

params.name: format_provider
params.doc: The string describing the format. You are allowed to use {0} as a format placeholder. The placeholder will be filled up with a semi-colon separated list: 0;0;2;4

params.name: separator
params.doc: Character to use for element separators. The default separator is a semi-colon.

methods.name: FromString
methods.summary: Try to deserialize a GH_Path from a String.

methods.name: Increment(Int32)
methods.summary: Increment a specific index in this path by one.

params.name: index
params.doc: Index to increment. Valid indices are (-Length+1) to (Length-1).

returns.doc: A new path with incremented index.

methods.name: Increment(Int32, Int32)
methods.signature: public GH_Path Increment(int index, int offset)
methods.summary: Increment a specific index in this path by one.

params.name: index
params.doc: Index to increment. Valis indices are (-Length+1) to (Length-1).

params.name: offset
params.doc: Amount to increment.

returns.doc: A new path with incremented index.

methods.name: IsAncestor
methods.signature: public bool IsAncestor(GH_Path potential_ancestor, ref int additional_generations)
methods.summary: Test another path to see if it is an ancestor of this path. For a path to be considered an ancestor, it must share the initial dimensions.

params.name: potential_ancestor
params.doc: Parental path to test.

params.name: additional_generations
params.doc: This value will hold the number of generations between parent and child path.

returns.doc: True if other overlaps completely with this path while being at least one element shorter.

methods.name: IsCoincident(Int32[])
methods.summary: Test to see if this path is coincident with set of integers.

params.name: index_list
params.doc: Integers to compare to.

returns.doc: True if both paths are coincident (identical), false if not.

methods.name: IsCoincident(GH_Path)
methods.summary: Test to see if this path is coincident with another path.

params.name: other
params.doc: Path to compare to.

returns.doc: True if both paths are coincident (identical), false if not.

methods.name: PrependElement
methods.summary: Create a new path by prepending a new index value to this path.

params.name: index
params.doc: Index value to prepend.

methods.name: Read
methods.summary: Read this path from an archive.

params.name: reader
params.doc: Object to read with.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SplitPathLikeString
methods.signature: public static bool SplitPathLikeString(string s, out string[] path_segments, out string index_segment)
methods.summary: Try to split up a path-like formatted string "{A;B;C}(i)" into component parts.

params.name: s
params.doc: String to parse.

params.name: path_segments
params.doc: Output - Strings inside the path portion of s.

params.name: index_segment
params.doc: Output - String inside the index portion of s.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: ToString()
methods.summary: Concatenates the indices in the path.

methods.name: ToString(Boolean)
methods.summary: Concatenates the indices in the path.

params.name: includeBrackets
params.doc: If True, brackets will be prepended and appended to the path string.

methods.name: Write
methods.summary: Write this path to an archive.

params.name: writer
params.doc: Object to write with.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: Equality
methods.signature: public static bool operator ==(GH_Path A, GH_Path B)

methods.name: GreaterThan
methods.signature: public static bool operator >(GH_Path A, GH_Path B)

methods.name: Inequality
methods.signature: public static bool operator !=(GH_Path A, GH_Path B)

methods.name: LessThan
methods.signature: public static bool operator <(GH_Path A, GH_Path B)

properties.name: Dimension
properties.summary: Gets or sets the value of the path at the given dimension index.

properties.name: Indices
properties.summary: Gets or sets the entire index space; the path that identifies an element in structure space. You should not change the index space when the path is used inside a structure since it will invalidate the sort order. If you don't know what you're doing, for Pete's sake don't touch this.

properties.name: InternalPath
properties.summary: Gets or sets the entire index space; the path that identifies an element in structure space. You should not change the index space when the path is used inside a structure since it will invalidate the sort order. If you don't know what you're doing, for Pete's sake don't touch this.

properties.name: Length
properties.summary: Returns the number of dimensions in the path.

properties.name: Valid
properties.summary: Gets whether this path is valid. Invalid paths either have no elements or negative elements.
